claimText: The Net-Zero Framework for Global Shipping was approved by the International Maritime Organization (IMO) with a target of achieving net-zero emissions by around 2050.
evidenceExcerpt: The International Maritime Organization (IMO) approved the Net-Zero Framework for Global Shipping, aiming for net-zero emissions close to 2050.
examUse: Statement-based question on the agency responsible and target year.

claimText: The Net-Zero Framework for Global Shipping will be incorporated into Annex VI of the MARPOL Convention.
evidenceExcerpt: The Framework will be included in Annex VI of MARPOL (International Convention for the Prevention of Pollution from Ships).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on environmental conventions.
distractorHooks: Annex I instead of Annex VI

claimText: The Net-Zero Framework for Global Shipping applies mandatorily to large ocean-going ships over 5,000 gross tonnage.
evidenceExcerpt: It applies to large ocean-going ships over 5,000 gross tonnage (emitting 85 percent of international shipping CO2).
distractorHooks: Applies to all ships regardless of tonnage
Applies to ships over 10,000 gross tonnage

claimText: The Net-Zero Framework for Global Shipping is the first framework to combine mandatory emissions limits and greenhouse gas pricing across an entire sector.
evidenceExcerpt: It is the first framework to combine mandatory emissions limits and GHG pricing across an entire sector.
examUse: Feature-based question on the uniqueness of the framework.
distractorHooks: It relies solely on voluntary emission limits
It applies only to specific shipping routes rather than the entire sector

claimText: Compliance with the Net-Zero Framework for Global Shipping requires adherence to a Global Fuel Standard to reduce greenhouse gas fuel intensity (GFI).
evidenceExcerpt: Compliance requires adhering to a Global Fuel Standard to reduce greenhouse gas fuel intensity (GFI) and a Global Economic Measure involving remedial units and financial rewards.
distractorHooks: Requires adherence to a Global Fleet Standard
Focuses only on carbon offsetting rather than fuel intensity
